meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't deploy apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Deploy Apps Fast for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Use this SaaS starter kit checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your client projects faster than ever.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting server configurations and deployment bottlenecks. A professional SaaS starter kit abstracts the infrastructure, providing a pre-configured, production-ready environment. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for automated implementation, you can bypass manual setup and focus entirely on delivering high-value features to your clients.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and you’re stuck in a 'dependency hell' loop. You’ve spent three days wrestling with environment variables, database migrations, and cloud permissions instead of building the actual product. This is the silent killer of freelance momentum: the inability to bridge the gap between local prototype and live production.

But the era of manual infrastructure management is over. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you transform deployment from a technical hurdle into a repeatable, automated process. We’re going to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your development workflow into a high-speed assembly line.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Select a robust boilerplate to handle authentication, billing, and database ORM out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new SaaS project using a modern Next.js and Supabase stack with pre-configured authentication and Stripe integration.

steps.title: Automate Environment Configuration
steps.description: Standardize local and production environments to prevent 'it works on my machine' errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure .env.example file and a script to validate required environment variables before deployment.

steps.title: Database Schema Mapping
steps.description: Quickly define your data models to ensure your SaaS backend is scalable from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application including Users, Subscriptions, and Projects tables.

steps.title: Deploy CI/CD Pipelines
steps.description: Implement automated testing and deployment triggers to ensure every push is production-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, tests, and deploys to Vercel upon merging to the main branch.

steps.title: Final Production Sanity Check
steps.description: Perform a last-pass audit of security headers and performance optimizations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current build for security vulnerabilities and suggest optimizations for faster load times.

pro_tips: Always use a monorepo structure to keep your shared logic separate from client-specific UI code.
Never hardcode secrets; use a vault service like Doppler to sync environment variables across your team.
Implement feature flags early so you can deploy code to production without exposing unfinished features to clients.
Use database migrations instead of manual SQL dumps to ensure production data integrity.
Monitor your deployment logs using an automated tool to catch 500-errors before your client does.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, focus on setting up automated billing notifications and user analytics to provide your client with a complete business dashboard.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint to stop struggling with deployments and start shipping profitable SaaS products. Stop wasting time on infrastructure and start focusing on your clients. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
